quality_notes: Add vulnerability: mention a mistake, failure, or lesson learned. Try 'I was wrong', 'took me years to learn', or 'finally understood'.
Remove naked structural labels like 'Best Practices:' or 'Common Mistakes:' — these are prompt scaffolding that leaked into the post. Write the insight directly.
Include one fenced visual block that matches the planned diagram type.
Keep the post in the LinkedIn sweet spot: roughly 140 to 340 words.

post_text:
📌 AI in Math Solutions

I used to think AI could solve complex math problems on its own —
it can't, at least not without significant integration effort.

Most engineers try to force-fit AI-driven solutions into existing workflows —
— they should instead design workflows around the strengths of AI 🤖.

Best Practices: Iterate on feedback
Common Mistakes: Overreliance on automation

💬 What's the biggest challenge you face when integrating AI into math workflows?

#Mathematics #ScaleEngineering #ProductionEnvironments #ComplexProblems #HybridModeling #HumanAIcollaboration 💡📊👥💻
